game_name: Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles IV: Turtles in Time

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Tutorial combat - simple melee enemies establish basic attack mechanics
Introduces armored enemy variant requiring repeated hits or powered attacks
Multiple consecutive hits unlock damage boost against tougher enemies
Ability to use environment - slam foot soldiers into surrounding enemies for crowd control
Fly form requires timed attacks; establishes boss pattern recognition
Thematic enemy from TMNT II film; group encounter
Narrative device introducing historical era levels and escalating difficulty
Period-specific bosses: Cement Man, Tokka/Rahzar, Leatherhead - increasing difficulty
Confined space, constrained movement, moderate enemy density
UFO pilot form; only boss with dialogue in English versions - climactic mid-boss
Culmination of all learned mechanics; restores Statue of Liberty upon victory

typical_tension_triggers: Time warp transition to historical eras
Increasing enemy density in later levels
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Wave-based escalation with progressive difficulty through era transitions; tension builds through cumulative enemy encounters then peaks at boss fights; time warp narrative device marks clear difficulty inflection point

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction to core combat mechanics (basic attacks, power attacks, throws) against foot soldiers and stone warriors; Baxter Stockman establishes boss pattern expectations
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Transition through pizza monsters and cement enemies; time warp introduces historical era variety; Tokka/Rahzar and Leatherhead bosses escalate required skill
difficulty_curve.late_game: Super Krang UFO form and final Shredder encounter demand mastery of all mechanics; Technodrome elevator sequence in SNES version adds spatial constraint challenge
notable_spikes: Time warp transition (narrative and difficulty shift)

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard street-level encounters with multiple weaker enemies; teaches crowd management through slam mechanics and power attacks
After time warp, each era introduces period-appropriate bosses with escalating complexity; temporal setting serves as difficulty signifier
Elevator fight sequence in Technodrome (SNES exclusive) restricts lateral movement; forces vertical spacing management
Single champion fights with pattern-recognition requirements; punctuate wave-clearing sections to reward learned mechanics
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All boss stages (Baxter Stockman through Shredder)

sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - TCRF article on TMNT IV
Wikipedia article on Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles: Turtles in Time
